channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: UN official denies seeing 'a shred of evidence' showing staff in Gaza held hostages
item.description: Tom Fletcher, a top UN humanitarian aid official, says he has not seen "a shred of evidence" linking the international body's workers in Gaza to Hamas hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN: Sudan war is world's worst humanitarian crisis: 30 million need aid, 16 million of them kids
item.description: The head of the U.N. children's agency says the war in Sudan has created the world’s largest and most devastating humanitarian crisis, with over 30 million people needing aid this year – 16 million of them children.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump signals cautious optimism on Russia accepting Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: President Trump on Thursday said he hoped Russia would “do the right thing” as his administration presses for a ceasefire agreement between Moscow and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Trump pushes annexation of Greenland during meeting with NATO secretary
item.description: President Trump on Thursday expressed confidence the United States would annex Greenland, even suggesting the head of the NATO alliance could be a key player in facilitating the acquisition.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: UN report accuses Israel of sexual violence against Palestinians. Netanyahu claims anti-Israel bias
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — United Nations-backed human rights experts on Thursday accused Israel of “the systematic use of sexual, reproductive and other gender-based violence” in its war with Hamas in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Peru's detained ex-President Castillo transferred to a hospital days after starting hunger strike
item.description: Former Peruvian President Pedro Castillo has been taken from prison to a hospital days after beginning a hunger strike in protest of his ongoing rebellion trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Putin thanks Trump for principles of ceasefire push, but does not say yes
item.description: Russian President Putin says he agrees in "principle" with President Trump's desire for a 30-day ceasefire but argued it would only help Ukraine and said he wants a lasting peace plan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin says he agrees in principle with US proposal for 30-day ceasefire in Ukraine
item.description: “So the idea itself is correct, and we certainly support it,” Putin told a news conference in Moscow.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pakistan's leader meets survivors of deadly train hijacking and the commandos who ended the siege
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistan’s prime minister on Thursday commended the country’s armed forces for successfully rescuing 339 passengers after a deadly train hijacking by insurgents in the southwest. A total of 25 people were killed by the attackers.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Poland calls on US to place nukes within its borders amid Russia threat
item.description: Polish President Duda has once again called on the US to place its nuclear arms within its borders to better deter Russia, mirroring talks with France amid security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration resumes immigrant family detentions
item.description: The Trump administration has resumed family detention of immigrants, a legal nonprofit said, reigniting a controversial practice largely curtailed under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration empties Guantánamo of migrants, flies all back to US
item.description: The Trump administration’s plans to detain migrants in Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, hit another roadblock this week after all 40 such individuals held there were sent back to the United States, with no future deportation flights scheduled to the island.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Pope receives hundreds of well-wishes on the 12th anniversary of his papacy, marked in the hospital
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has marked the 12th anniversary of his pontificate in the hospital as he received increasingly positive medical news on his recovery and hundreds of messages wishing him well.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump threatens 200% tariff on European wine after proposed whiskey tariff
item.description: Trump vowed a new escalation in his trade war if the EU pushes ahead with the planned 50% tariff on American whiskey.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Steve Witkoff has gone from spotting Trump cash at a deli to helping him navigate Ukraine and Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Trump special envoy Steve Witkoff arrived in Moscow on Thursday for high-level talks aimed at ending Russia's invasion of Ukraine, the latest high-profile assignment for the real estate magnate turned White House foreign policy fixer.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump threatens Europe with 200 percent tariff on wine, Champagne
item.description: President Trump on Thursday threatened to put a hefty 200 percent tariff on wine, Champagne and other alcohol coming in from Europe if the European Union (EU) does not remove a tariff on whiskey.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Belgian prosecutors arrest suspects in Huawei bribery probe targeting EU Parliament
item.description: Belgian federal prosecutors say they've arrested several people as part of a corruption probe linked to the European Parliament and Huawei.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ethiopia's Tigray regional government appeals for help after factional fighters seize key towns
item.description: ADDIS ABABA, Ethiopia (AP) — The interim government of Ethiopia’s Tigray region appealed for the Ethiopian federal government to intervene after a faction of the Tigray People's Liberation Front seized control of two major towns, leaving several people wounded and raising fears of a return to civil
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL GETACHEW, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump meets with NATO chief Mark Rutte
item.description: The meeting comes after Trump called on European allies to contribute more to the alliance's defense fund.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Naples struck by 4.4-magnitude quake causing minor damage and light injuries
item.description: ROME (AP) — The southern Italian city of Naples was struck by a 4.4-magnitude quake early Thursday that caused only minor damage and sent 11 people to the hospital, the most serious suffering contusions after part of a ceiling collapsed, officials said.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: UN-backed experts accuse Israel of sexual and gender-based violence in Gaza
item.description: United Nations-backed human rights experts allege Israel has caused “disproportionate violence against women and children” during the war against Hamas in Gaza,.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: President Duda calls on US to place nuclear weapons in Poland
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Poland's President Andrzej Duda again called on the United States to deploy nuclear weapons to Poland as a deterrent to Russia, the latest indication that the frontline NATO nation is increasingly considering nuclear protection as fears of Russia grow.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Argentine retirees and soccer fans clash with police during protests against austerity measures
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — Argentine retirees and fans of several soccer teams clashed with police Wednesday during a protest in front of Congress in the capital, Buenos Aires, against the economic policies implemented by the government of President Javier Milei.
item.dc:creator: SARA ESPAÑA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICC takes custody of former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte in crimes against humanity case
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The International Criminal Court took custody of former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte on Wednesday on a warrant accusing him of crimes against humanity for deadly anti-drugs crackdowns he oversaw while in office.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An Emirati diplomat identified as having a letter from Trump meets with Iran's foreign minister
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — An Emirati diplomat earlier identified by Tehran as carrying a letter from U.S. President Donald Trump seeking to jump-start talks over Iran's rapidly advancing nuclear program met Wednesday with Iran's foreign minister in the Iranian capital.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistan says insurgents who attacked a passenger train killed 21 hostages while all others freed
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Insurgents who attacked a passenger train carrying 440 passengers in restive southwestern Pakistan killed 21 hostages before security forces killed all 33 of the assailants, and all other passengers were rescued, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Congo and M23 rebel group will hold peace talks on Tuesday, mediator Angola says
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — The government of Congo will hold peace talks next week in Angola with the Rwanda-backed M23 rebel group that has captured key areas of Congo’s mineral-rich east, mediator Angola announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lutnick: Canada becoming US state 'best way' to merge economies
item.description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said Wednesday that Canada becoming a U.S. state is the “best way” to merge the two countries’ economies. “The best way, the president has said it, the best way to actually merge the economies of Canada and the United States is for Canada to become our 51st state.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump envoys begin Russia outreach after Ukraine breakthrough
item.description: Trump administration officials are reaching out to their Russian counterparts as the White House says the ball is in Moscow’s court to agree to a ceasefire in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Trump says tariffs will be 'positive'; Vance pushes Russia for ceasefire
item.description: “The United States of America is going to take back a lot of what was stolen from it by other countries.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Russia hits Zelenskyy's hometown as Ukraine signals it's ready for peace
item.description: As Ukraine appeared to pivot towards peace, Russia launched a deadly attack on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's hometown of Kryvvi Rih.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greenland's center-right party pulls off upset victory as Trump seeks control
item.description: Greenland's center-right Demokraatit party saw a sharp increase in support as it won the country's election on Tuesday. Independence became a central issue as President Donald Trump talked of a U.S. takeover.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Austria's new government is stopping family reunions immediately for migrants
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — The new Austrian government said Wednesday that family reunion procedures for migrants will be immediately halted because the country is no longer able to absorb newcomers adequately.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and PHILIPP JENNE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Detained captain of cargo ship that collided with US oil tanker is Russian national, ship owner says
item.description: A Russian national was revealed to be the captain of the cargo ship that collided with an American tanker off the coast of England, spilling U.S. military jet fuel into the sea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia waiting for US-Ukraine cease-fire proposal details, Kremlin says
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov told reporters Wednesday that Russia must get the details on U.S.-Ukrainian cease-fire talks before commenting on the proposal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australia won't retaliate against 'unjustified' US tariffs on steel and aluminum
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said Wednesday that U.S. tariffs on Australian steel and aluminum were unjustified, but his government would not retaliate with its own tariffs. U.S.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis follows Vatican spiritual retreat as doctors say he's no longer in imminent danger
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis participated remotely in the Vatican's spiritual retreat Tuesday after getting good news from his doctors: They upgraded his prognosis and say he is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of the respiratory infection that has kept him hospitalized for nearly a mon
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
